content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with atom labels. Let me examine atom 22 (C:22) carefully to identify its neighbor atoms.

From the image, I can clearly see:
- Atom C:22 (carbon, index 22) is the central atom in focus
- It is directly bonded to:
  - H:23 (hydrogen, index 23) - visible as a white sphere connected to C:22
  - H:24 (hydrogen, index 24) - visible as a white sphere connected to C:22
  - C:25 (carbon, index 25) - visible as an orange sphere connected to C:22
  - N:19 (nitrogen, index 19) - visible as a blue sphere connected to C:22

The connections are clearly visible in the 3D structure, with C:22 having four covalent bonds forming a tetrahedral geometry.

Based on the file name "geomconf_8317.xyz", the geomconf_index for this molecule is 8317.

The neighbor atoms of atom 22 (C) are:
- Atom 19: N (nitrogen)
- Atom 23: H (hydrogen)
- Atom 24: H (hydrogen)
- Atom 25: C (carbon)

These are all the atoms covalently connected to atom 22.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 22, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
